title: Kentrophyllum rhiphaeum
taxonomy: Kentrophyllum rhiphaeum belongs to the family Santalaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as mistletoe or sandalwood. The genus Kentrophyllum is part of the order Santalales, which includes various species of parasitic or hemiparasitic plants. These plants are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions, and are known for their unique relationships with host plants. Kentrophyllum rhiphaeum is a species of flowering plant that is native to certain parts of the world. Further classification of Kentrophyllum rhiphaeum would require more specific information about its taxonomy.
